title: Short-Horizon Trade Imbalance Predictor
short_description: SIMD-optimized nowcast engine for sub-microsecond next-tick direction predictions using order flow features
long_description: SIMD-optimized nowcast engine for sub-microsecond next-tick direction predictions using order flow features
technical_details:
## Algorithm & Strategy Structure

Nowcasts next-tick mid-price direction using a real-time order flow feature vector. The nowcast model is a shallow ridge regression trained on 50,000 historical ticks with features updated each tick.

Feature vector (12 components):
- OBI at levels 1–5
- Rolling 1s, 5s, 10s net trade imbalance
- Order arrival rate ratio (bid vs ask side)
- Mid-price velocity (Δmid over last 10 ticks)

## Signal Generation

```
signal = w^T · x_t
```

If `|signal| > threshold_bps` (in basis points of the current spread), submit an aggressive order in the predicted direction.

SIMD AVX2 intrinsics compute the full dot product across all 12 features in under 50ns.

## Prediction Calibration

Model is recalibrated every 5 minutes using a sliding window of recent ticks. Calibration uses isotonic regression to convert raw scores to probabilities.

## Metric Methodology

- **Directional accuracy**: Fraction of predictions where next-tick mid moves in the predicted direction.
- **Brier score**: Measures calibration quality of probability estimates.
- **Expected PnL per prediction**: Signal magnitude × realized mid-move, averaged across all predictions above threshold.
- **Sharpe**: Computed on per-trade PnL with 0.1 bps taker cost deducted per trade.
